body.title: Primitive completeness survey: what do you need to build X entirely from habits?
body.result: Survey complete. primitives.json v2: 21 documented primitives (was 9; 12 existed in code but weren't cataloged). 16 gaps identified across 3 target programs (book_reader, worker_monitor, cc_design_loop). Gap ranking: prim_cc_post + prim_log needed by 2-3 programs (XS effort, implement first); prim_wg_ingest is the hardest gap (new_semantics, book reader can't work without it); prim_thalamus_parse + prim_habit_update are new_semantics/M effort, lowest priority. book_reader needs 5 new primitives; worker_monitor needs 6; cc_design_loop needs 5. Catalog also removed stale 'missing' entries for prim_count + prim_format (covered by prim_list_count + prim_str_format).
body.description: Survey ticket, not implementation. Pick 3 target programs: book reader, worker monitor, CC design loop. For each: enumerate every primitive needed. Classify each as: (a) already exists, (b) thin code_ref wrapper, (c) needs new semantics/design. Output: updated primitives.json + gap list ranked by how many target programs need it. This answers when the system is sufficient rather than discovering gaps one program at a time.
